howItWorks.intro: This marine fuel consumption calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Fuel Used = Burn Rate × Trip Duration
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: How much fuel does a boat use per hour?
faq.answer: It varies enormously: sailboat under power 1-3 GPH, pontoon boat 3-8 GPH, center console 8-20 GPH, cruiser 15-40 GPH, sportfisher 30-80+ GPH. Fuel consumption scales roughly with displacement and speed.

faq.question: What is the 1/3 fuel rule?
faq.answer: The 1/3 rule: use 1/3 of fuel going out, 1/3 returning, and keep 1/3 in reserve for emergencies. This means your usable range is based on only 1/3 of your tank capacity.

faq.question: How do I calculate nautical miles per gallon?
faq.answer: NMPG = Speed (knots) ÷ Fuel Consumption (GPH). For example, 25 knots using 15 GPH = 1.67 NMPG. Boats are far less fuel-efficient than cars, especially at high speeds.

faq.question: Is the Marine Fuel Consumption Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard boating and marine formulas trusted by boaters and sailors. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the Marine Fuel Consumption Calculator?
sections.body: The Marine Fuel Consumption Calculator is a free online tool designed for boaters and sailors who need quick, accurate calculations in the boating and marine space. By entering your fuel burn rate, cruising speed, trip distance, you get instant results including trip duration, fuel used, trip fuel cost. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting trip duration right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In boating and marine, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by boaters and sailors worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to navigate safely with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your fuel burn rate and need to find the right trip duration. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Fuel Burn Rate (gallons/hour)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your fuel burn rate — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: cruising speed, trip distance, fuel price, fuel tank size. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your trip duration, fuel used, trip fuel cost. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your fuel burn rate before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both trip duration and fuel used — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to navigate safely.
If you're unsure about your fuel tank size, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
